stories.headline: Netanyahu orders Israeli forces to take control of 70% of Gaza

stories.headline: US and Iran reach tentative deal, pending Trump's approval
stories.summary: The US and Iran have agreed to extend the ceasefire by 60 days to make space for further negotiations to end the war but Trump still has to approve the deal, reports said Thursday, citing US officials familiar with the matter.

stories.headline: Israel’s Netanyahu directs army to seize 70 percent of Gaza Strip
stories.summary: Israeli army has already expanded its control of Gaza by 11 percent over the 'Yellow Line', beyond terms of 'ceasefire'.

stories.headline: Gaza City hospitals say several killed in strike, as Israel targets Hamas leaders

stories.headline: EU sanctions ‘extremist’ Israeli settlers in occupied West Bank
stories.summary: European Union says sanctioned individuals and groups have violated the rights of Palestinians.

stories.headline: Iran says it targeted American base after fresh US strikes
stories.summary: The hostilities come during a fragile ceasefire between the US and Iran, and protracted negotiations to end the three-month war.

stories.headline: Bessent Says Trump Won’t Accept a Bad Deal on Iran: Full Briefing
stories.summary: Treasury Secretary Scott Bessent said that President Trump will not make a bad deal with Iran amid reports of a 60-day truce extension waiting the president's approval. Bessent, speaking at the White House press briefing, outlined President Trump's conditions for a deal with Iran, saying that for a deal to happen the country must agree to open the Strait of Hormuz, turn over its enriched uranium and not to pursue a nuclear weapon. (Source: Bloomb

stories.headline: After stern words from Trump, U.S. and Iran trade strikes
stories.summary: The renewed violence came after the president said Iran was wrong if it thought he would feel pressure to make concessions and resolve the standoff before U.S. midterm elections later this year.

stories.headline: EU will never be a neutral mediator between Ukraine and Russia, says Kallas
stories.summary: The High Representative has dismissed the buzz over a special envoy for talks with Russia, arguing the EU will "never" be a neutral mediator between Moscow and Kyiv. "We have been clearly on Ukraine's side," she said.

stories.headline: US justice department launches criminal investigation into Trump accuser E Jean Carroll
stories.summary: The case centres on whether the former columnist lied about the funding she received for her civil lawsuit against the US president.

stories.headline: Russia on the back foot with dynamics of war shifting in Ukraine’s favour, EU says – as it happened

stories.headline: Israel hits Lebanese capital in 'targeted strike'
stories.summary: Beirut had until now largely been spared even as both Israel and Hezbollah accused each other of breaking last month's ceasefire.

stories.headline: How Cuba is addressing its housing crisis with shipping containers
stories.summary: BBC's Will Grant visited Barrio Toledo, where at least 700 containers are being repurposed into two-bedroom homes with kitchens, bathrooms and patios.

stories.headline: Chevron CEO warns oil prices to jump over summer as supplies dwindle
stories.summary: Blockade in Strait of Hormuz has removed up to 13mn barrels a day from global markets and drawn down ‘shock absorbers’

stories.headline: Zscaler CEO: Anthropic's Mythos Has Added 'Fuel to the Fire'
stories.summary: Zscaler CEO Jay Chaudry expects says the company's track record is 'very good' when it comes to revenue, and that Anthropic's Mythos has added 'fuel to the fire' on cybersecurity.. The security software company gave a fourth-quarter revenue forecast earlier this week that was weaker than expected. Evercore downgrades the stock, noting slowing net new customers.  (Source: Bloomberg)

stories.headline: Where Do E. Jean Carroll’s Lawsuits Against President Trump Stand?
stories.summary: Two monetary judgments against Mr. Trump are winding their way through the legal system, with the Supreme Court likely to be the final word on both.

stories.headline: Michigan Governor Gretchen Whitmer Will Not Run for President in 2028
stories.summary: Governor Whitmer of Michigan had been viewed as part of a field of possible Democratic candidates. She has been governor for two terms, and is barred from running again.

stories.headline: Judge Declines, for Now, to Block Mail-In Voting Changes Ordered by Trump
stories.summary: The ruling allowed the Trump administration to continue to pursue changes that include enlisting the Postal Service in checking voters against a national database.

stories.headline: James Talarico Opens Texas Senate Campaign Against Ken Paxton With Houston Rally
stories.summary: Personal attacks and a packed campaign rally set the tone for what is likely to be a rancorous contest in Texas for U.S. Senate between James Talarico and Ken Paxton.

stories.headline: Massie Visits Greene in Costa Rica for Fishing and ‘Spicy’ Political Talks
stories.summary: The two Republican critics of President Trump — Thomas Massie, who lost his House primary last week, and Marjorie Taylor Greene, who resigned from Congress — met up in the tropics.

stories.headline: Justice Dept. Is Said to Open Criminal Inquiry of E. Jean Carroll, Who Accused Trump of Rape
stories.summary: Ms. Carroll, who prevailed in a civil trial after accusing President Trump of sexual abuse, is the latest target in a Justice Department campaign going after his perceived enemies.

stories.headline: Michigan Gov. Gretchen Whitmer says she won’t run for president in 2028
stories.summary: Whitmer, term-limited after two gubernatorial wins in her battleground state, had been widely viewed as a potential Democratic candidate.

stories.headline: Clashes between armed groups in Colombia kill at least 52
stories.summary: Rival groups are vying for territorial control of strategic cocaine production and trafficking regionAt least 52 guerrilla fighters have been killed in clashes between two rival armed groups vying for territorial control of a strategic cocaine production and trafficking region in south-east Colombia, a faction of the Revolutionary Armed Forces of Colombia (Farc) involved in the fighting has said.The clashes, the most violent in recent months, too

stories.headline: Japan's car exports to war-hit Middle East plummet 90% in April
French parliament inches towards symbolic repeal of 'Code noir' slavery legislation
stories.summary: France abolished enslaving humans more than 170 years ago and in 2001 recognised slavery and the slave trade as "crimes against humanity."

stories.headline: Canada signs landmark LNG energy deal with Germany
stories.summary: The deal would see one million tons of natural gas shipped annually from Canada to Germany by the early 2030s through a proposed terminal in British Columbia.

stories.headline: Musk’s tweet undermines SpaceX’s claims about Anthropic data centre deal
stories.summary: Billionaire says the arrangement, described in IPO filings as a three-year agreement, only lasts for 180 days

stories.headline: Worthington Steel Raises $1.4 Billion for Klöeckner Takeover
stories.summary: Worthington Steel Inc. raised $1.4 billion in the leveraged-finance market Thursday to help fund its acquisition of German metals company Klöeckner & Co SE.

stories.headline: Drugmaker Apotex Files for Canada IPO Showing Revenue Jump
stories.summary: Canadian generic drugmaker Apotex Health Corp. filed for an initial public offering in Toronto, it said in a statement on Thursday.

stories.headline: Can the OpenAI and Anthropic IPOs Live Up to Expectations?
stories.summary: The AI dynamos are eyeing the public markets, but some wonder whether SpaceX’s planned June listing will steal their thunder.

stories.headline: Hormel Foods Shares Rise on Strong Q2 Earnings
stories.summary: Hormel Foods reported adjusted earnings per share and organic sales for the second quarter that exceeded analyst expectations, driving a notable increase in its stock price. John Ghingo, President of Hormel Foods, discussed the company's ongoing turnaround plan and its positive impact on results. He speaks with Romaine Bostick and Katie Greifeld on "The Close." (Source: Bloomberg)

stories.headline: FTC Chair Ferguson Says Agency Probing Fertilizer Industry
stories.summary: The Federal Trade Commission recently launched an antitrust investigation into the rising costs of fertilizer in the US, the agency’s head said at an event in Texas on Thursday.

stories.headline: SpaceX Fever Accelerates Space ETFs, Weakness In Crypto ETF Flows | ETF IQ 5/28/2026
stories.summary: "Bloomberg ETF IQ" focuses on the opportunities, risks and current trends tied to the trillions of dollars in the global exchange traded funds industry. Today's guests: Strategas ETF Strategist Todd Sohn, TMX VettaFi Head of Sector & Industry Research Roxanna Islam, AdvisorShares CIO & Portfolio Manager Dan Ahrens, and PIMCO Head of Leveraged Finance David Forgash. (Source: Bloomberg)

stories.headline: Canada Fintechs Wealthsimple, Questrade to Offer Pre-IPO Access
stories.summary: Canadian online brokerages Wealthsimple and Questrade Inc. are joining a wave of companies promising to offer access to firms planning initial public offerings, as startup funding rounds heat up.

stories.headline: Swallowing Concerns, G.O.P. Senators Rally Behind Paxton
stories.summary: After attacking the Texas attorney general, who won the G.O.P. nomination, Senate Republicans are pivoting sharply to support him in what is now a competitive race.

stories.headline: Family accuse UK government of lack of support over death of Briton in Grenada

stories.headline: Image of Thai police in sparkly dresses with handcuffed suspect turns out to be AI fake
stories.summary: Picture was created by administrator in charge of station’s Facebook account who wanted to create ‘friendlier image’It was an arresting image and an irresistible story. stories.headline: Greener pasture of a shepherd’s life lures Chinese workers penned in by ‘996’ jobs

stories.summary: Datacentres used 22% of country’s electricity last year, pushing up household bills, study suggestsEnergy demand by datacentres in Ireland has added hundreds of euros to household electricity bills in a pattern that could be replicated across Europe, according to a report.Ireland’s growing number of datacentres last year used 22% of the country’s electricity, more than all urban homes combined, according to the Central Statistics Office. stories.headline: What’s behind South Africa’s anti-migrant protests?
stories.summary: Thousands of foreign workers are under increasing pressure to leave the country.

stories.headline: Latvia parliament approves new gov’t after drone dispute toppled coalition
stories.summary: Latvia's government reform ensures a pro-Western path as regional drone incidents heighten security concerns.

stories.headline: Yemen’s former leader Abd-Rabbu Mansour Hadi dies in exile at 80
stories.summary: Hadi was the internationally recognised president of Yemen who led a fractured government mostly from exile.

stories.headline: Oil Prices Steady as Traders Assess US-Iran Truce Reports
stories.summary: Oil prices were little changed after a volatile trading day as traders digested news that the US and Iran have reached a tentative deal to extend a ceasefire and work toward a pact to end the war.

stories.headline: Almost 500,000 Russian soldiers killed in Ukraine war, GCHQ says
stories.summary: GCHQ's director Anne Keast-Butler set out the threats facing the UK in her inaugural public speech.

stories.headline: 'Like a prisoner being released' - Relief for Iranians as internet shutdown ends
stories.summary: The government imposed the blackout after the start of the war with the US and Israel in February, cutting most Iranians off from the outside world.

stories.headline: Stocks Hit Record Highs After US-Iran Deal Reports: Markets Wrap

stories.summary: The searches come amid a series of scandals affecting Prime Minister Pedro Sánchez's PSOE.

stories.headline: The world's carmakers are struggling to compete with China

stories.summary: Dong Guangping, an activist and former police officer, spent hours at sea before reaching Korean waters.

stories.summary: With the US pulling out of trilateral talks with Russia and Ukraine, the EU is looking for potential candidates to step in.

stories.summary: The court said the man poisoned the tycoon for sidelining him after he helped secure a deal with Netflix for the 3 Body Problem.

stories.headline: Deal with US not imminent, Iran says
stories.summary: The US secretary of state earlier said that an agreement could possibly come on Monday.

stories.headline: Moment of gas explosion at China coal mine
stories.summary: At least 82 people have been killed and two are missing after a gas explosion at a coal mine in northern China on Sunday, officials say.

stories.headline: Anger grows after China's deadliest coal mining disaster in years
stories.summary: On China's tightly-controlled internet, people are calling for justice and questioning how this happened.

stories.headline: Bolivian minister's convoy ambushed while overseeing roadblock clearance
stories.summary: Security forces were met by resistance while attempting to clear highway barriers, amid unrest over the government's economic policies.

stories.headline: I survived a missile strike in the Strait of Hormuz, but my friend has not been found
stories.summary: Sunil Puniya was on his first sea job when a missile struck the oil tanker Skylight at the start of the Iran war.

stories.headline: Rosenberg: Luhansk strike sparks Russian accusations and vow to retaliate
stories.summary: Eighteen people were killed and 42 others injured in the strike on the town in Russian-occupied eastern Ukraine.

stories.headline: From AI to interceptors, Ukraine is trying to drone-proof its skies
stories.summary: After four years of Russia's full-scale invasion, Ukraine has become far more successful at fighting off air raids.
